steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure and the rule
steps.content: The sentence has an independent clause — “The conference’s goals were threefold” — followed by a series of three parallel infinitive phrases (“to ensure…, to secure…, and to advocate…”). The rule: a colon may introduce a list or elaboration only when it follows a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• "threefold : to": Places a colon after the complete independent clause, correctly introducing the series of infinitive phrases. ✓
  • "threefold. To": Violates the sentence-fragment rule — the period cuts off the independent clause and leaves “To ensure…to advocate” as a standalone fragment. ✗
  • "threefold to": Violates the run-on rule — the main clause and the series are fused with no punctuation. ✗
  • "threefold ; to": Violates the semicolon rule — a semicolon joins two independent clauses; it cannot introduce a list or series following a single clause. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
